@graph.schema:legalName: Sarasota Bay Listening Network - Passive Acoustic Listening Stations
@graph.schema:name: Sarasota Bay Listening Network - Passive Acoustic Listening Stations
@graph.schema:description: The Sarasota Bay Listening Network is a collaboration between biologists, engineers, educators, and citizens to explore the acoustic environment of Sarasota Bay.  We aim to 1) better understand the biology of Sarasota Bay’s marine life and how it responds to disturbances, such as red tide and boat noise, 2) communicate our findings to the public and in scientific forums, and 3) provide research opportunities for students. The first listening station was installed in 2017 and has grown to 13 acoustic listening stations. The network is supported by five partner institutions: Chicago Zoological Society’s Sarasota Dolphin Research Program, New College of Florida, Loggerhead Instruments, Eckerd College, and Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution.

schema:geo.schema:description: Bounding box polygon with lat long (Y X) coordinate order.

schema:funding.schema:description: Mote Scientific Foundation, Disney Conservation Fund, Rick and Nancy Moskovitz Foundation, and the Chicago Zoological Society
